model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Iowa State is still the clear pick because the overall talent, seed-line expectation, and program-level quality gap over Tennessee State are substantial, even with the Cyclones losing Keshon Gilbert for the tournament. Tennessee State has a credible underdog path after a strong 23-9 season and OVC title run, especially if Iowa State’s offense sputters without Gilbert, but the Tigers face a much steeper class jump here. Iowa State’s defensive baseline and overall roster strength still make it the much safer choice to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced team-quality edge outweighs injury concern
reasoningStep.summary: Iowa State’s injury situation lowers confidence somewhat, but the Cyclones still project as the clearly stronger team than Tennessee State and remain the safer Round of 64 pick.
evidence: ESPN reporting indicates Iowa State will be without star guard Keshon Gilbert for the NCAA tournament, which is the main factor reducing confidence.
Tennessee State enters 23-9 and won the Ohio Valley Conference tournament, giving the 15-seed a legitimate momentum-based upset path.
Tennessee State is making its first NCAA tournament appearance since the 1993-94 season, highlighting the jump in competition level.
Even accounting for Gilbert’s absence, Iowa State retains a major seed, talent, and defensive-profile advantage over Tennessee State.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.tennessean.com/story/sports/college/2026/03/15/tennessee-state-iowa-state-march-madness-bracket-ncaa-tournament/89091960007/ and extracted 2377 characters of text.

result.text: Tennessee State March Madness opponent is Iowa State in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Sports Titans Predators Nashville SC Vanderbilt UT High Schools Other Colleges Sports Columnist Joe Rexrode NFL Draft Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA TSU basketball’s March Madness opponent is Iowa State to open NCAA tournament bracket Mike Organ Nashville Tennessean Show Caption Hide Caption Tennessee State's basketball reacts to hearing name on Selection Show Tennessee State's basketball players, surrounded by fans at Gentry Center, cheered after hearing they will face Iowa State in the NCAA Tournament. Tennessee State will play Iowa State in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. TSU secured its spot by winning the Ohio Valley Conference Tournament against Morehead State. This is the Tigers' first NCAA Tournament appearance since the 1993-94 season. Tennessee State basketball will face Iowa State in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. The Tigers (23-9) earned an automatic berth in March Madness by winning the Ohio Valley Conference Tournament . TSU defeated Morehead State 93-67 after earning a double-bye into the conference tournament by winning the regular season championship. Iowa State (27-7) is the No. 2 seed in the Midwest Region. TSU (23-9) is the No. 15 seed. They will play on March 20 (1:50 p.m. CT, CBS) in St. Louis. BUY TSU BASKETBALL TICKETS FOR MARCH MADNESS This is TSU's first time in the NCAA Tournament since 1993-94 and only its third time since becoming an NCAA Division I program. In his first year as Tennessee State's coach, Nolan Smith was the OVC Co-Coach of the Year after leading the Tigers to their best regular season record as an NCAA Division I team. TSU heads into March Madness on a six-game win streak, its longest since the 2017-18 season. The Tigers have won nine of their last 10 games. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Current game label: Midwest 2/15
Round: Round of 64

Prior committed picks: 35
